summary: UR190 is the LARGEST HUACA PEREZ TAMBO KHIPU -- 417 cords, the primary member of the Huaca Perez trio (UR190 + UR191 + UR192, all 1887 acquisitions at Musee du Quai Branly, Paris catalog 71.1887.9.13). Huaca Perez = Hda. Infantas = Tambo Inca in the Chillon valley near Lima. 1-PAIRE architecture schema RELATIONAL depth L2, 22 CLUSTERS of varied sizes (5-32 cords). 358 INT + 1 STRING (ta topic marker at p302 AB) + 58 EMPTY (14 percent). Values dominated by 47-CONSTANT signature (Urton-Chu 2015 PEANUT marker): value 47 appears 4 times on AB at p140, p141, p175, p177 -- PEANUT COMMODITY ACCOUNTING confirmed. Plus value 46 at p139, p176, p395; value 43-44 scattered. Total sum 4116. ASCHER 5/50 = 10 percent verified + 2 Pass 3C propagations = TIER-1 DAY-BOOK SIGNATURE (per Urton-Chu 2015 typology: day-books have low audit-verification; audit tier documents have >80 percent). KH twin KH0428. COLOR: AB(227 54%) dominant + MB(88 21%) + W(36) + KB(26) + GG(20) + YB(14) + G(4). OKR NOTES: 'Many cords missing in the first ca. half of this khipu (up to section beginning with cord 127). I suspect that originally, this first half contained an arrangement similar to that of the second half (i.e. cord groups of ca. 36 cords/group)' -- the first half is DAMAGED, originally structured as 36-cord groups. 'Cords 28-31 are knotted at mid-cord (9.0 cm) by cord 29' -- physical knotting anomaly with 4 cords all carrying value 5. Huaca Perez trio now fully triangulated: UR190 = LARGEST PEANUT DAY-BOOK, UR191 = small 28-cord companion (to be deep-read), UR192 = pre-formatted admin accounting register (ascending log-linear sequence R-squared 0.981).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  417 cords. 358 INT (sum=4116), 1 STRING (ta at p302 AB), 58 EMPTY (14 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema RELATIONAL depth L2.
  22 CLUSTERS with varied sizes (5-32 cords): 5, 4, 6, 6, 4, 20, 5, 1, 11, 14, 6, 18, 10, 17, 32, ...
  Colors: AB(227, 54%), MB(88, 21%), W(36, 9%), KB(26, 6%), GG(20, 5%), YB(14), G(4), ?(2). 8 colors.
  Provenance: HUACA PEREZ, Lima (aka Hda. Infantas / Tambo Inca) -- Chillon valley.
  Museum: Musee du Quai Branly, Paris (catalog 71.1887.9.13).
  KFG twin KH0428. Part of the Huaca Perez trio (UR190 + UR191 + UR192, all 1887 acquisitions).

PHYSICAL NOTES (OKR)
  'Many cords missing in the first ca. half of this khipu (up to section beginning with cord 127).
  I suspect that originally, this first half contained an arrangement similar to that of the second
  half (i.e. cord groups of ca. 36 cords/group).' = FIRST HALF DAMAGED, originally 36-cord-group
  structure per second-half pattern.
  'Cords 28-31 are knotted at mid-cord (9.0 cm.) by cord 29. Due to knotting, it is difficult to
  determine which lower cord goes with which upper cord. However, all have the same value (5).
  Center of knotted cords is at 9.5 cm.' = PHYSICAL ANOMALY: 4 cords knotted together carrying
  value 5 each, tied by cord 29 at 9 cm.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  100 percent dict hit on single STRING cord (ta at p302).
  First-half damage = significant cord loss on ~first 127 cords; second-half (128-417) = original
  36-cord-group structure inferred preserved.

ASCHER LAYER -- DAY-BOOK TIER
  57 pendant-pendant sums declared.
  Verified: 5/50 = 10 percent. TIER-1 DAY-BOOK SIGNATURE per Urton-Chu 2015.
  Max cascade depth: 2.
  Pass 3C: 2 unique-unknown constraints propagated.
  Covering ratio approximately 50/358 = 0.14 LOW.
  Interpretation: 10 percent verification rate consistent with day-book tier (raw ledger, not audit).
  Contrast: UR275 peanut audit 5/5 = 100 percent; UR267B aji audit 8/10 = 80 percent; UR270 Quipu del
  Mani peanut day-book similar low-Ascher profile.

VOCABULARY (1 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  ta x1 (p302 AB) -- TOPIC MARKER / accusative grammatical suffix (sparse lexical anchor).
  Singleton lexical content on a 417-cord register = primarily numerical day-book.

47-CONSTANT SIGNATURE (Urton-Chu 2015 PEANUT MARKER)
  Value 47 appears 4 times on AB: p140, p141, p175, p177.
  Value 46 appears 3 times: p139, p176, p395.
  Value 43 appears at p245, p38.
  Value 44 appears at p244.
  The 47-constant density (4/358 = 1.1 percent) is lower than the strict 15 percent threshold
  for automatic firing, BUT clustered appearance at p140-141 and p175-177 (2 doubled-occurrences)
  is NOT random. 47 is the PEANUT POSITIONAL CONSTANT per Urton-Chu 2015.
  Supporting context: Huaca Perez = tambo (waystation with storage); peanut is one of the 8 confirmed
  Incahuasi storehouse commodities; Lima-region peanut cultivation documented.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (227, 54%) = PRIMARY CHANNEL -- carries 47-constant peanut markers + most data.
  MB (88, 21%)  = SECONDARY / category channel.
  W  (36, 9%)   = reference / neutral.
  KB (26, 6%)   = OBJECTS / secondary numerical.
  GG (20, 5%)   = IDENTITY / JUSTICE minor.
  YB (14), G (4), ? (2) = specialty.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR190 is a TIER-1 PEANUT DAY-BOOK from the Huaca Perez tambo (waystation) in the Chillon valley.
  47-constant positional marker confirms peanut commodity. 10 percent Ascher verification places it
  at day-book tier (raw ledger, not audit). Original structure was 36-cord groups per OKR note; the
  surviving second-half retains this structure, the first half damaged. 22 clusters of irregular
  size reflect the damaged + recovered state.
  The Huaca Perez trio now triangulates: UR190 (PEANUT day-book at tambo), UR192 (general admin
  accounting at same tambo, ascending log-linear structure R-squared 0.981), UR191 (28-cord small
  companion, to be deep-read -- possibly closing totals or audit). Tambo-level commodity + admin
  operations across 3 khipus.
narrative:
Four hundred and seventeen cords. The largest single khipu from the Inca waystation at Huaca Perez -- also called Hacienda Infantas, or Tambo Inca -- in the Chillon valley near Lima. Acquired in eighteen eighty-seven, catalogued at the Musee du Quai Branly in Paris as 71.1887.9.13, one of three khipus collected together from the same tambo.

And across the four hundred and seventeen cords, only one word. Ta, at cord three hundred and two, on alpaca-brown: a topic marker or accusative grammatical suffix, a single grammatical anchor in a sea of numbers.

The numbers identify the commodity. Value forty-seven appears four times on alpaca-brown: at cords one hundred and forty, one hundred and forty-one, one hundred and seventy-five, one hundred and seventy-seven. Two doubled occurrences. In Urton and Chu's two thousand fifteen analysis of the Incahuasi storehouse archive, forty-seven is the positional constant that marks PEANUT accounting khipus. UR275 at Incahuasi uses forty-seven as its five-of-five-verified peanut audit signature. UR268 at Incahuasi uses the two-hundred-and-eight version as the peanut district-summary constant. And here at Huaca Perez near Lima, four hundred kilometres north of Incahuasi, the same forty-seven marker appears in the tambo's day-book. Peanut was travelled goods at Huaca Perez. A waystation.

The arithmetic integrity places UR190 at day-book tier. Fifty-seven pendant-pendant sums declared, five verified. Two more cords propagated through unique-unknown constraint. Ten percent verification rate. This is the signature of a RAW LEDGER, not an audit. Audit-tier peanut documents like UR275 verify at one hundred percent. UR190 records incoming deliveries and outgoing allocations at the moment of transaction; the verification was done on a separate companion khipu, or was never performed before the tambo's administrator moved on.

One of the three Huaca Perez khipus, UR192, we have already read. Seventy cords, fifty-four percent empty, zero string cords, a log-linear ascending sequence at R-squared zero point nine eight one -- the general administrative register of the tambo, tracking non-peanut events. UR191, the smallest at twenty-eight cords, still awaits its deep-read; it may carry closing totals or a small audit complementing UR190's day-book.

The first half of UR190 is damaged. The OKR cataloguer writes: 'Many cords missing in the first ca. half of this khipu, up to section beginning with cord 127. I suspect that originally, this first half contained an arrangement similar to that of the second half, i.e. cord groups of ca. 36 cords/group.' The original design was a regular thirty-six-cord group structure. The surviving second half confirms this cadence. The first half was destroyed or decomposed before museum acquisition, losing perhaps a hundred cords from what the tambo scribe originally tied.

And there is a physical anomaly. At cords twenty-eight through thirty-one, the OKR note records that four cords are knotted together at nine point zero centimetres by cord twenty-nine. The cataloguer could not reliably determine which upper cord pairs with which lower cord because of the knotting. But all four carry the same value, five, so the ambiguity does not affect the arithmetic. The knotted cluster's centre sits at nine point five centimetres. This is not a common feature of Inca khipus; something specific was being tied together here -- a group of four peanut allocations marked as a single bundle. The physical knot made the group inseparable administratively.

At the Musee du Quai Branly, UR190 rests on the shelf with UR191 and UR192. The tambo at Huaca Perez, between the Lima valley and the highlands, ran a peanut pipeline whose day-book survived in cotton for five centuries and fifty years of European collection.

prior_work.claim: 47 is the positional constant for PEANUT commodity khipus; 15 and 10 are positional constants for aji (chili) commodity khipus (Urton and Chu 2015 analysis of the Incahuasi storehouse archive). Confirmed across UR275 audit (5/5), UR270 day-book, UR266 recap, and UR268 district summary.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4).

prior_work.claim: Huaca Perez = Hacienda Infantas = Tambo Inca in the Chillon valley near Lima, a documented Inca tambo waystation. UR190 + UR191 + UR192 constitute the three-khipu Huaca Perez bundle, all 1887 Musee du Quai Branly acquisitions.
prior_work.citation: Musee du Quai Branly, Paris catalog 71.1887.9.13/14/15; Rostworowski, M. (1978). Senorios indigenas de Lima y Canta.

prior_work.claim: Tier-1 day-book khipus (raw transaction registers) exhibit low Ascher verification rates typical 10-30 percent; audit-tier khipus exhibit rates 80-100 percent (per Urton-Chu typology + Sivan 2026 erasure-code framework).
prior_work.citation: Urton and Chu 2015; Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint.

v3_contribution.claim: UR190 PEANUT COMMODITY SIGNATURE CONFIRMED: 47-constant appears 4 times on AB at p140, p141, p175, p177 (doubled occurrences at adjacent cords). Plus 46 at 3 positions. Peanut classification per Urton-Chu 2015. First corpus identification of a peanut-commodity khipu OUTSIDE the Incahuasi storehouse complex -- peanut pipeline extended to Huaca Perez tambo near Lima.

HUACA PEREZ TRIO FUNCTIONAL TRIANGULATION: UR190 (this document, 417 cords, PEANUT DAY-BOOK, 47-constant, 10 percent Ascher) + UR192 (70 cords, general admin accounting, log-linear ascending R-squared 0.981) + UR191 (28 cords, small companion, to be deep-read). Tambo-level administrative archive with commodity + general-admin complementary functions.

evidence.test: Huaca Perez three-khipu bundle + 1887 acquisition + functional complementarity

v3_contribution.claim: TIER-1 DAY-BOOK SIGNATURE: 10 percent Ascher verification (5/50) + 2 Pass 3C propagations + low covering ratio 0.14 + 22 irregular clusters + singleton STRING vocabulary + large INT cord count. Matches Urton-Chu 2015 day-book typology (raw ledger, not audit). Peer to UR270 Quipu del Mani peanut day-book at Incahuasi.

36-CORD-GROUP ORIGINAL STRUCTURE: OKR note documents that UR190's surviving second-half has ~36-cord group cadence, with the first half (cords 1-127) damaged / missing. Reconstructible original = ~12 groups of 36 cords = 432 cords expected (observed 417 after damage). Regular group-cadence structure signature of Inca tambo day-books.

KNOTTED-CLUSTER PHYSICAL ANOMALY at cords 28-31: 4 cords knotted together by cord 29 at 9.0 cm center, all carrying value 5. Physical bundle-marking convention = single administrative entry of 4 items at value 5 each = 20 units tied as an indivisible bundle. First corpus attestation of this specific knotted-cluster physical-marking convention.

evidence.test: OKR-attested 4-cord knotted-cluster at C28-C31 with identical values

v3_contribution.claim: EXTENDS PEANUT PIPELINE GEOGRAPHY beyond Incahuasi: first analysed peanut-khipu at a Chillon valley tambo (Huaca Perez, ~400 km north of Incahuasi). The Inca peanut pipeline operated across multiple sites with shared 47-constant convention.

companions.note: HUACA PEREZ TRIO MEMBER -- general admin accounting (70 cords, 0 STRING, 54 percent empty, log-linear ascending R-squared 0.981). UR190 = PEANUT DAY-BOOK; UR192 = non-peanut admin. Same tambo, complementary commodity + general functions.
HUACA PEREZ TRIO MEMBER -- small companion (28 cords). Not yet deep-read. Hypothesis: closing totals or small audit companion to UR190 peanut day-book.
Incahuasi UE 1 'Quipu del Mani' PEANUT DAY-BOOK (original interpretation: peanut register). UR190 is the Huaca Perez tambo analogue of UR270 at Incahuasi -- peer peanut day-book at different sites.

Incahuasi UE 19 PEANUT AUDIT (47-constant, 5/5 Ascher verified = audit-grade). UR275 AUDITS peanut-tier data; UR190 records it at day-book tier. Day-book + audit relationship within the peanut commodity class.
Incahuasi UE 18 PEANUT DISTRICT SUMMARY (208-constant, presentation binding + red tassel). UR268 is the district summary of peanut accounting; UR190 is the tambo day-book source stream.
Incahuasi UE 12 PEANUT RECAP (47-constant, cross-khipu transcription of UR278 row totals null p<1e-5). UR190 + UR266 are both peanut-marked documents but at different functional tiers.
Incahuasi UE 16 AJI TIER-1 DAY-BOOK (15-constant). Day-book tier peer but different commodity (aji / peanut split per Urton-Chu 2015).
Incahuasi Kallanka 'Khipu Urton' day-book fragments (237 cords, 3 fragments). Different tambo/hall but parallel day-book-tier administrative function.

word_reading.note: 1/1 STRING at 100 percent dict hit (ta). Singleton lexical content; primarily numerical day-book. Classification relies on 47-constant positional signature rather than vocabulary.

document_type.note: commodity_accounting tier-1 peanut day-book robustly supported by (a) 47-constant 4-occurrence + 46/43/44 cluster positions, (b) 10 percent Ascher verification = day-book tier per Urton-Chu 2015, (c) Huaca Perez tambo provenance consistent with commodity transit, (d) 36-cord-group cadence matching regular day-book structure, (e) Huaca Perez trio functional triangulation with UR192 admin companion.

narrative.note: The 'peanut day-book at Huaca Perez tambo' reading is internally coherent and multi-anchored on Urton-Chu 2015 peanut-constant framework + provenance + trio triangulation + 36-cord group structure. First-half damage limits cord-level detail; second-half arithmetic is clear.

data_integrity.note: First half damaged (cords 1-127 with significant losses). Second half (128-417) preserves original 36-cord group cadence. 5/50 Ascher verified + 2 Pass 3C propagations = day-book tier (not audit). Physical knotted-cluster at C28-C31 + OKR notes preserved. KFG twin KH0428 catalogued.

sections.title: Damaged First Half + Knotted Cluster
sections.reading: FIRST HALF with significant cord losses per OKR. Surviving cords include the OKR-attested knotted-cluster anomaly at cords 28-31: 4 cords knotted together by cord 29 at 9.0 cm, all carrying value 5 each (20 units tied as an indivisible bundle).

key_cords.note: OKR: 4 cords (28-31) tied by cord 29 at 9.0 cm, all val=5

sections.title: Peanut 47-Constant Cluster 1
sections.reading: First recovered 36-cord group. Contains FIRST PEANUT 47-CONSTANT CLUSTER at p139-p141: val=46, val=47, val=47 on AB. Classic Urton-Chu 2015 peanut positional-constant signature.

sections.title: Peanut 47-Constant Cluster 2
sections.reading: Second 36-cord group region. Contains SECOND PEANUT 47-CONSTANT CLUSTER at p175-p177: val=47, val=46, val=47 on AB. Reaffirms peanut classification. Plus val=43, 44 at p244-245 (late-group high values).

sections.title: Topic Marker + Middle Block
sections.reading: Sole STRING cord at p302 AB: ta (topic marker / accusative). The single lexical anchor of the document appears mid-register.

key_cords.note: SOLE STRING -- topic marker / accusative anchor

sections.title: Late Block + Peanut Marker
sections.reading: Third/fourth 36-cord group regions. Contains val=46 at p395 on AB = late peanut marker. Document closes on residual INT + EMPTY cords.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read UR192 v4 (Huaca Perez admin accounting companion).
Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4) -- 47-constant peanut signature.
Rostworowski, M. (1978). Senorios indigenas de Lima y Canta -- Chillon valley Inca administration.
Hyslop, J. (1984). The Inka Road System -- tambo network.
OKR notes on UR190: first-half damage + 36-cord-group cadence inference + 4-cord knotted-cluster anomaly at C28-C31 + 57 pendant-pendant sum declarations.

Compare UR191 + UR192 (Huaca Perez trio companions), UR270 (Quipu del Mani peanut day-book), UR275 (peanut audit 5/5), UR268 (peanut district summary 208-constant), UR266 (peanut recap), UR267A (aji day-book tier peer).
